items.question: In a typical workflow, what does `git pull` combine?
items.correctAnswer: A fetch plus merging into the current branch
items.explanation: `git pull` downloads updates and then integrates them, commonly by merge unless configured otherwise.

items.question: What does Git store for each commit besides the snapshot of files?
items.correctAnswer: A pointer to its parent commit(s)
incorrectAnswers: A copy of the remote URL
The diff from the empty tree only
items.explanation: Each commit links to one or more parents, which is how Git builds history as a graph.

items.question: What does `git rebase` do to commits compared with merge for integrating work?
items.correctAnswer: Replays commits on top of another base to linearize history
incorrectAnswers: Deletes all commits on the branch permanently
Creates a second copy of every blob in the repo
Only updates remote-tracking refs without changing commits
items.explanation: Rebase moves or reapplies commits so the branch history reads as if it started from a newer point.

items.question: What does a merge conflict mean in Git?
items.correctAnswer: Git cannot auto-merge overlapping edits and needs a human fix
incorrectAnswers: The remote rejected your SSH key
items.explanation: Conflicts happen when the same region of a file changed differently on two histories.

items.question: What is a lightweight tag in Git?
items.correctAnswer: A name that points directly to a commit object
incorrectAnswers: A tag that stores a full extra commit message object always
A branch that tracks a remote by default
A signed certificate for HTTPS remotes
items.explanation: Lightweight tags are simple refs to commits, unlike annotated tags which are separate objects with metadata.

items.question: What is the purpose of the staging area (index) in Git?
items.correctAnswer: To choose which changes go into the next commit
incorrectAnswers: To run automated tests before push
To encrypt secrets in the repository
items.explanation: You stage changes with add so the next commit records exactly what you selected.
